name: Ulldecona Reservoir and Moleta del Terme Loop
name_original: Parque Natural de la Tinença de Benifassà — Circular por el Embalse de Ulldecona y la Moleta del Terme
description: Hike a moderate 5.6-mile (8.9 km) loop in Parque Natural de la Tinença de Benifassà, featuring Ulldecona Reservoir and Moleta del Terme view
region_intro: Waterfall hiking trails around Embassament d'Ulldecona are set within the Ports de Beseit natural park, characterized by limestone mountains, deep ravines, and Mediterranean forests. The landscape features dramatic topography with natural pools and rock walls. Riparian forests thrive along permanent water sections, adding to the diverse terrain. This region offers a variety of trails suitable for exploring its natural features. Best waterfall hiking trails around Embassament d'Ulldecona The most popular waterfall hiking route is Portell de l'Infern – Salt de Robert Waterfall loop from Parc Natural de la Tinença de Benifassà, a 9.4 miles (15.2 km) difficult trail that takes 5 hours to complete. This route features significant elevation gain through varied terrain. Another top favourite among local hikers is Casa Forestal de la Tenalla – Salt de Robert Waterfall loop from Parc Natural de la Tinença de Benifassà, a moderate 8.4 miles (13.5 km) path. It offers views of the natural park and leads to the Salt de Robert waterfall. Local hikers also love the Ombries del Salt de Robert Trail, a 2.7 miles (4.3 km) trail leading through forested areas, often completed in about 1 hour 36 minutes. Hiking around Embassament d'Ulldecona is defined by limestone mountains, deep ravines, and Mediterranean forests. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy walks to more challenging ascents. The routes in Embassament d'Ulldecona are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 160 reviews. More than 1000 hikers have used komoot to explore Embassament d'Ulldecona's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many waterfall hiking trails are there around Embassament d'Ulldecona?
A: There are 5 dedicated waterfall hiking trails around Embassament d'Ulldecona that have been curated by the komoot community. These routes primarily lead to the picturesque Salt de Robert Waterfall, offering diverse experiences within the Ports de Beseit natural park.

Q: Are there easy waterfall hikes suitable for families?
A: Yes, there is at least one easy waterfall hike, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e880413771/ombries-del-salt-de-robert-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ombries del Salt de Robert Trail&lt;/a&gt;, which is 4.3 km long and can be completed in about 1 hour 36 minutes. This trail leads through forested areas, making it a pleasant option for families.

Q: What is the best time of year to visit the waterfalls?
A: The best time to visit the waterfalls around Embassament d'Ulldecona is typically during spring or after periods of significant rainfall. This ensures the waterfalls are at their most impressive. Spring also brings blooming native plants, adding vibrant color to the landscape of the Ports de Beseit natural park.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on waterfall hikes in this region?
A: The terrain around Embassament d'Ulldecona is characterized by limestone mountains, deep ravines, and lush Mediterranean forests. You can expect varied paths, from forested sections to routes that navigate through gorges with natural pools and rock walls. Some trails, like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/5317855" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Portell de l'Infern – Salt de Robert Waterfall loop&lt;/a&gt;, feature significant elevation gain.

Q: Are there any circular waterfall routes available?
A: Yes, several waterfall hikes are circular. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/23754234" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Casa Forestal de la Tenalla – Salt de Robert Waterfall loop&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 13.5 km path, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/1675541" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mas del Pixón – Salt de Robert Waterfall loop&lt;/a&gt; is an 8.1 km moderate route. These loops allow you to experience the diverse scenery without retracing your steps.

Q: What natural features or landmarks can I see along the waterfall trails?
A: Beyond the waterfalls themselves, the trails offer views of the stunning Ports de Beseit natural park, with its impressive rocky walls and rich biodiversity. You might encounter natural pools, dramatic ravines like the Barranc de la Fou, and riparian forests with willow and oleander. While not directly on every waterfall trail, the region also features the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/212629" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mirador del Pantano&lt;/a&gt; for panoramic reservoir views and the highest point, Mont Caro, offering breathtaking vistas.

Q: Are the waterfall hiking trails dog-friendly?
A: While specific regulations for dogs on these exact waterfall trails are not detailed, the Ports de Beseit natural park generally welcomes dogs, often requiring them to be on a leash to protect wildlife. It's always advisable to check local signage or park regulations before heading out with your dog.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about hiking near Embassament d'Ulldecona?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 160 reviews. Hikers often praise the tranquility of the region, the stunning natural scenery, and the opportunity to connect with nature away from urban life. The diverse terrain and picturesque waterfalls are frequently highlighted as key attractions.

Q: Are there any challenging waterfall hikes for experienced hikers?
A: Yes, for experienced hikers seeking a challenge, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/5317855" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Portell de l'Infern – Salt de Robert Waterfall loop&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 15.2 km trail with significant elevation gain, typically taking around 5 hours to complete. This route offers a demanding yet rewarding experience through varied terrain.

Q: How long do the waterfall hikes typically take?
A: The duration of waterfall hikes varies depending on the trail's length and difficulty. Shorter, easier routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e880413771/ombries-del-salt-de-robert-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ombries del Salt de Robert Trail&lt;/a&gt; can take around 1 hour 36 minutes. Moderate loops, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/23754234" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Casa Forestal de la Tenalla – Salt de Robert Waterfall loop&lt;/a&gt;, typically require about 3 hours 55 minutes, while difficult trails can extend to 5 hours or more.

Q: Is there parking available near the waterfall trails?
A: While specific parking locations for each individual waterfall trail are not detailed, the region around Embassament d'Ulldecona and within the Ports de Beseit natural park generally offers designated parking areas for hikers. It's recommended to check the starting points of specific routes on komoot for more precise parking information.
tips: Located in the municipality of Puebla de Benifasar, the Ulldecona reservoir receives water from the Fou ravine (or Sénia river), the Teulatí ravine, the Tenalla ravine and the Pobla ravine (or riu Verd). The views from the bridge are magnificent and the color of the water varies according to the weather, with emerald or turquoise shades.

From the top, at 947 meters above sea level, Moleta del Terme offers amazing views of the entire Ports valley and Bajo Maestrat. Getting there requires physical effort and a firm step given some technical sectors, but the views will undoubtedly be a great reward!

faqs.question: Where does the trail start and is there parking available?
faqs.answer: The trail typically starts near the Ulldecona Reservoir. Parking is generally available in designated areas around the reservoir, which serves as a common access point for this circular route. Arriving earlier in the day is advisable, especially during peak seasons, to secure a spot.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this circular route?
faqs.answer: The terrain is varied, offering a moderate hiking experience. You'll encounter easily-accessible paths, sections of dirt track parallel to the road near the reservoir, and transitions into pine and oak forests. Expect some uneven and rocky sections, particularly as you ascend towards Moleta del Terme. Hiking boots are highly recommended for comfort and stability.

faqs.question: What can I see along the route?
faqs.answer: This route is rich in natural beauty and panoramic views. Key highlights include the stunning Ulldecona Reservoir, known for its emerald or turquoise waters. From the summit of Moleta del Terme, you'll enjoy breathtaking panoramic views of the entire Ports valley and the Bajo Maestrat. The Mirador del Pantano also offers excellent vistas of the reservoir and surrounding mountains. You'll also pass through well-conserved woodlands and impressive rocky walls.

faqs.question: Is the Parque Natural de la Tinença de Benifassà dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Yes, the Parque Natural de la Tinença de Benifassà generally allows dogs on trails. However, it's crucial to keep your dog on a leash at all times to protect the local wildlife, especially given the presence of Iberian ibex, roe deer, and various bird species. Always practice Leave No Trace principles, including picking up after your pet.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or pay an entrance fee to hike in the Parque Natural de la Tinença de Benifassà?
faqs.answer: No, there is typically no entrance fee or permit required to access the trails within the Parque Natural de la Tinença de Benifassà, including this circular route. However, as it is a protected natural park, visitors are expected to respect all park regulations, such as staying on marked paths, not disturbing wildlife, and taking all trash with them.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike this trail?
faqs.answer: The best times to hike this trail are generally spring and autumn. During these seasons, the weather is milder, making for more comfortable hiking conditions. Spring brings lush greenery and wildflowers, while autumn offers beautiful fall colors. Summer can be very hot, so if hiking then, it's best to start early in the morning. Winter can be pleasant, but higher elevations might experience colder temperatures.

faqs.question: Is this trail suitable for beginners or families?
faqs.answer: This specific route is classified as moderate, requiring good fitness due to its length and elevation gain. While parts are easily accessible, some sections are uneven and rocky. For beginners or families with young children, other, shorter and easier routes around the Ulldecona Reservoir might be more suitable. This particular circular trail is better for those with some hiking experience.

faqs.question: What kind of wildlife can I expect to see in the Parque Natural de la Tinença de Benifassà?
faqs.answer: The Parque Natural de la Tinença de Benifassà is known for its rich biodiversity. You have a good chance of spotting Iberian ibex, which are quite common in the area. Other wildlife includes roe deer, wild boar, and various birds of prey such as vultures, Bonelli's eagles, and booted eagles. Keep an eye out, especially in quieter sections of the trail.

faqs.question: Does this trail intersect with other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, sections of this route overlap with other paths. Approximately 44% of the trail follows part of the [CIMA CS01] Fredes route, and about 6% coincides with the Camí de la Fou. This allows for potential extensions or variations if you wish to explore more of the park's extensive network of trails.

faqs.question: Are there any cafes or restaurants near the trailhead?
faqs.answer: While the immediate vicinity of the Ulldecona Reservoir is primarily natural, you can find cafes and restaurants in nearby villages within the Tinença de Benifassà region. It's a good idea to check opening hours in advance, especially outside of peak tourist seasons, or pack your own snacks and drinks for the hike.

faqs.question: What should I pack for this hike?
faqs.answer: Given the moderate difficulty and varied terrain, it's recommended to pack plenty of water, snacks, and wear sturdy hiking boots. Layers of clothing are advisable as temperatures can change, especially when gaining elevation. Sun protection (hat, sunscreen) is important, and a map or GPS device is useful for navigation. Don't forget your camera for the stunning views!
